sections.heading: Risk Factors
sections.content:
Germline BRCA1/2 pathogenic variants: Found in approximately 5% of all breast cancer patients. Associated with increased risk of contralateral breast cancer and hereditary breast cancer syndrome. Genetic counseling recommended for patients at high risk as defined by NCCN Guidelines for Genetic/Familial High-Risk Assessment [BINV-1]. BRCA1/2 PV carriers have higher rates among HER2-negative disease [BINV-Q 2]. Strength: Strong (category 1 for genetic testing consideration)
Germline PALB2 pathogenic variants: PALB2 PVs confer breast cancer susceptibility. Olaparib has shown activity in patients with germline PALB2 PV (ORR 75%, clinical benefit rate 83.3%, median PFS 9.4 months) [BINV-Q 7, MS-83]. Strength: Moderate (category 2A for olaparib use)
TP53 pathogenic variants (Li-Fraumeni syndrome): Known genetic predisposition to breast cancer. Patients with Li-Fraumeni syndrome (germline TP53 mutation) have an increased risk of phyllodes tumors [PHYLL-1]. TP53 PV carrier is a relative contraindication to breast-conserving therapy (category 2B) [BINV-G]. Strength: Strong (relative contraindication to BCS)
Homozygous ATM mutation: Homozygous (biallelic) inactivation for ATM mutation often leads to ataxia-telangiectasia syndrome and is an absolute contraindication to breast-conserving therapy (category 2B) [BINV-G]. Strength: Strong (absolute contraindication to BCS)
Young age at diagnosis: Young age is a significant predictor of increased likelihood of ipsilateral breast tumor recurrence after breast-conserving treatment [MS-13]. Patients ≤35 years or premenopausal carriers of known BRCA1/2 mutation should consider additional risk reduction strategies [MS-13]. Strength: Moderate (increased local recurrence risk; consideration for risk reduction)
Pregnancy-associated breast cancer: Breast cancer occurring concurrently with pregnancy is associated with ALN-positive disease, larger primary tumor size, poorly differentiated histology, more frequent ER/PR negativity, and approximately 30% HER2-positivity. Diagnosis is often delayed [MS-91]. Strength: Moderate (higher stage at presentation)
Obesity: Obesity (BMI ≥30) is associated with increased risk of contralateral breast cancer in women with ER-positive tumors. Obesity is associated with poorer outcomes for certain subtypes of breast cancers [MS-53]. Strength: Moderate
Tobacco use/smoking: Smoking is associated with increased risk of contralateral breast cancer. Smoking increases perioperative complications for all types of breast reconstruction including wound healing, mastectomy skin flap necrosis, implant exposure, and infection [MS-26]. Strength: Moderate
Alcohol consumption: Alcohol consumption is associated with increased risk of contralateral breast cancer in women with ER-positive tumors [MS-53]. Strength: Moderate
Hormone replacement therapy: Menopausal hormone therapy is relatively contraindicated in survivors of hormonally mediated cancers; should be used with caution in those with increased genetic cancer risk [BINV-17]. Strength: Moderate (contraindicated in survivors)
Higher breast density: Higher breast density may affect detection and may warrant additional screening with breast MRI. Patients with personal history of breast cancer diagnosed at age ≤50 or with dense breasts should consider annual MRI [BINV-B]. Strength: Moderate (affects screening recommendations)

cards.detail: Found in approximately 5% of all breast cancer patients. Associated with increased risk of contralateral breast cancer and hereditary breast cancer syndrome. Genetic counseling recommended for patients at high risk as defined by NCCN Guidelines for Genetic/Familial High-Risk Assessment [BINV-1]. BRCA1/2 PV carriers have higher rates among HER2-negative disease [BINV-Q 2].
PALB2 PVs confer breast cancer susceptibility. Olaparib has shown activity in patients with germline PALB2 PV (ORR 75%, clinical benefit rate 83.3%, median PFS 9.4 months) [BINV-Q 7, MS-83].

Known genetic predisposition to breast cancer. Patients with Li-Fraumeni syndrome (germline TP53 mutation) have an increased risk of phyllodes tumors [PHYLL-1]. TP53 PV carrier is a relative contraindication to breast-conserving therapy (category 2B) [BINV-G].
Homozygous (biallelic) inactivation for ATM mutation often leads to ataxia-telangiectasia syndrome and is an absolute contraindication to breast-conserving therapy (category 2B) [BINV-G].

cards.strength: Moderate (increased local recurrence risk; consideration for risk reduction)
cards.detail: Young age is a significant predictor of increased likelihood of ipsilateral breast tumor recurrence after breast-conserving treatment [MS-13]. Patients ≤35 years or premenopausal carriers of known BRCA1/2 mutation should consider additional risk reduction strategies [MS-13].

Breast cancer occurring concurrently with pregnancy is associated with ALN-positive disease, larger primary tumor size, poorly differentiated histology, more frequent ER/PR negativity, and approximately 30% HER2-positivity. Diagnosis is often delayed [MS-91].

Obesity (BMI ≥30) is associated with increased risk of contralateral breast cancer in women with ER-positive tumors. Obesity is associated with poorer outcomes for certain subtypes of breast cancers [MS-53].

Smoking is associated with increased risk of contralateral breast cancer. Smoking increases perioperative complications for all types of breast reconstruction including wound healing, mastectomy skin flap necrosis, implant exposure, and infection [MS-26].
Alcohol consumption is associated with increased risk of contralateral breast cancer in women with ER-positive tumors [MS-53].
Menopausal hormone therapy is relatively contraindicated in survivors of hormonally mediated cancers; should be used with caution in those with increased genetic cancer risk [BINV-17].

Higher breast density may affect detection and may warrant additional screening with breast MRI. Patients with personal history of breast cancer diagnosed at age ≤50 or with dense breasts should consider annual MRI [BINV-B].
